# X Silly Feed

> A Chrome extension that transforms X.com (Twitter) into a silly, sketch-inspired newspaper aesthetic using the experimental `border-shape` CSS property.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): Primary project documentation and setup instructions.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): History of notable changes to the project.
- [Humans](humans.txt): Information about the creator and tools used.
- [Todo](tasks/todo.md): Current tasks and project roadmap.
- [Lessons](tasks/lessons.md): Lessons learned and patterns identified during development.
- [Security Audit](tasks/security-audit.md): Comprehensive security review of the project.

## Legal

- [License](LICENSE): Apache 2.0 license requiring proper attribution.
- [Privacy Policy](PRIVACY.md): Data handling and privacy compliance information.
- [Terms of Service](TERMS.md): Rules and conditions for using the extension.
- [Disclaimer](DISCLAIMER.md): Statement of non-affiliation with X/Twitter.

## Landing Page

- [Landing Page](docs/index.html): Themed landing page for the extension.

## Extension Configuration

- [Manifest](manifest.json): Extension manifest defining permissions, scripts, and assets.

## Source Code

- [Background Script](src/background/service_worker.js): Service worker for background tasks.
- [Content Script](src/content/content.js): Main logic injected into X.com pages.
- [Content Styles](src/content/styles.css): Styles defining the silly newspaper aesthetic.
- [Popup HTML](src/popup/popup.html): User interface for the extension's popup.
- [Popup Script](src/popup/popup.js): Interaction logic for the popup UI.
